phrases.intent_en: Ask where the open-air food market is set up
phrases.text_local: Où se tient le marché en plein air ce matin ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TYEHN luh mar-SHAY ahn plehn EHR suh mah-TEHN
phrases.cultural_note: The outdoor market on Rue Mouffetard operates Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday, and Sunday mornings (typically 8am–1pm). The lower section of the street near Place Saint-Médard is where the market stalls concentrate, selling cheese, produce, meat, fish, and wine.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the cheese vendor for a recommendation for today
phrases.text_local: Qu'est-ce que vous me recommandez comme fromage pour aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: kess kuh voo muh ruh-koh-mahn-DAY kohm froh-MAHZH poor oh-zhoor-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: The fromageries on Rue Mouffetard are excellent and vendors are happy to give tastings and advice. Ask for 'un fromage à pâte molle' (soft cheese) or 'un fromage affiné' (aged cheese). The street has been a food market since the Middle Ages.

phrases.intent_en: Buy a few slices of ham at the charcuterie
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais quelques tranches de jambon, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DREH kel-kuh TRAHNSH duh zhahm-BOHN, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Rue Mouffetard has several outstanding charcuteries. Try jambon de Bayonne or jambon cru — thinly sliced cured hams. Combined with a baguette and cheese from a nearby fromagerie, this is a perfect Parisian picnic for Jardin des Plantes nearby.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the history of the street
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que c'est vrai que cette rue date de l'époque romaine ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh seh VREH kuh set ROO daht duh lay-POK roh-MEN
phrases.cultural_note: Rue Mouffetard follows the route of the ancient Roman road from Paris (Lutetia) to Lyon. It is one of the oldest streets in Paris, and medieval houses with painted facades can still be seen. The painted sign 'Au Vieux Chêne' above one doorway dates to the 18th century.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a baguette at the bakery
phrases.text_local: Une baguette tradition, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: oon bah-GET trah-dee-SYOHN, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: The 'baguette de tradition française' is made without additives or frozen dough and has more flavor and a crunchier crust than a standard baguette. It's slightly more expensive but noticeably better. The bakeries on Rue Mouffetard regularly win awards for their baguettes.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a wine recommendation to go with cheese
phrases.text_local: Quel vin conseilleriez-vous pour accompagner un plateau de fromages ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel vehn kohn-seh-yuh-RYAY voo poor ah-kohm-pahn-YAY uhn plah-TOH duh froh-MAHZH
phrases.cultural_note: The wine shops on Rue Mouffetard stock wines from across France. For a cheese platter, ask for a Burgundy (Bourgogne) white for soft cheeses or a Côtes du Rhône red for aged cheeses. The shopkeepers are knowledgeable and enjoy making recommendations.
